rationale: Obligation requires post1_self_ == post2_self_ for StrictlyOrderedVec<K> (single private field v: Vec<K>). The antecedent provides only view equality (Seq<K>) and valid(). Verus has no view-injectivity axiom for Vec: confirmed by isolated tests that v1@==v2@ implies neither v1==v2, v1=~=v2, nor v1=~~=v2, even with broadcast use vstd::std_specs::vec::group_vec_axioms. Adding #[verifier::ext_equal] to the wrapper struct does not help, since the inner Vec field still lacks view-extensionality. Inside the same module, view body unfolds (post1_self_.v@==post2_self_.v@ is derivable), but the bridge to Vec equality is missing. Inserted a best-effort proof block that derives the seq-level equality. Closing struct equality would require an assume/axiom, which is forbidden.
